image_prompt: SVG: audiogram line graph — hearing threshold curve. X-axis Frequency / Hz on a log scale with ticks 15.6, 31.2, 62.5, 125, 250, 500, 1000, 2000, 4000, 8000, 16000; Y-axis Sound level / dB from -20 to 70 (step 10). Curve starts ~72 dB at 15.6 Hz, falls to a minimum (~ -5 dB) near 3000-4000 Hz, then rises again to ~24 dB at 16000 Hz. Labels 'Audible' (above curve) and 'Inaudible' (below curve).

SVG: anatomical cross-section of the human ear showing pinna (outer ear), ear canal, eardrum (tympanic membrane), middle ear bones (malleus/incus/stapes), cochlea (labelled spiral), and auditory nerve bundles. Skull bone speckled background; soft anatomical palette distinct from IB original.

SVG: cochlear implant illustration. Left panel: stylised photo-like image of person with external coil/processor behind ear. Right panel: anatomical cutaway showing external processor (a) on skin, internal implant (b) under skin, and electrode array (c) inside cochlea — drawn with dashed loop. Labels a, b, c in orange leader-circles.

SVG: line graph 'effect of altitude on quantity of oxygen in the blood'. X-axis Altitude / m 0-4000 (step 500); Y-axis Quantity of oxygen in blood / arbitrary units 0-90 (step 10). Two plotted points (700, 71) and (3500, 44) joined by a straight line — decreasing relationship.

SVG: line graph 'effect of altitude on hemoglobin concentration in the blood'. X-axis Altitude / m 0-4000 (step 500); Y-axis Hemoglobin concentration / g dm⁻³ 15.0-18.0 (step 0.5). Two plotted points (700, 15.3) and (3500, 17.3) joined by a straight line — increasing relationship.

topic: O2 saturation & heart rate vs altitude
image_prompt: SVG: two scatter panels side by side, five students measured at three altitudes (~700, ~2300, ~3500 m). Left panel: 'effect of altitude on blood oxygen saturation', X-axis Altitude / m 0-4000 (step 500), Y-axis Blood oxygen saturation / % 70-100 (step 5); saturation decreases with altitude (clusters ~94-100, ~89-96, ~84-88). Right panel: 'effect of altitude on heart rate', X-axis Altitude / m 0-4000 (step 500), Y-axis Heart rate / beats per minute 20-120 (step 10); heart rate increases with altitude (clusters ~53-82, ~70-95, ~79-108).

SVG: original illustration of a pika (small round-eared mountain mammal) crouched on a sunlit rock with rocky scree in the background. Sandy fur with prominent round ears, big dark eye, white whiskers. Distinct palette and composition from any IB original.

SVG: layered green mountain ridges against a blue sky with six labelled orange-rimmed circles each containing a pika icon placed at different altitudes along the slopes. Left side: 'Altitude / m:' axis with ticks 500–3000 every 500 m. Right side: 'Mean body mass of population:' label above a pika illustration on a digital weighing scale. 'Not to scale' caption bottom-right.

SVG: cartoon-style scene of an American chestnut tree with surrounding background trees. Two translucent zoom circles — blue showing the whole tree, green showing close-up of catkins/leaves — overlap to highlight characteristic features. Black caption bar: 'the American chestnut tree that is native to North America.'

SVG: two-panel illustration. Top: simplified outline of the contiguous United States filled with a grid of small green tree icons (chestnut range). Bottom: forest scene of mature chestnut trees with a bear walking across the grass under a blue sky, captioned 'Many animals, such as squirrels, bears and birds, relied on chestnut trees'.

SVG: two-panel illustration. Top panel: cartoon scene of a logger chopping a chestnut tree next to a log cabin with chimney, a stump beside him, and a sepia inset circle of historical loggers beside a giant tree — captioned 'Humans too used the chestnuts as a food source'. Bottom panel: two overlapping zoom circles showing American chestnut spiky green burrs (green circle) and young Japanese chestnut nuts on a branch (orange-red circle) over a forest path background.

SVG: two-panel illustration. Top: forest scene with two zoom circles (green = American chestnut spiky burrs, orange-red = Japanese chestnut young nuts on branch) over a forest path, captioned 'The imported trees carried a disease causing fungus,'. Bottom: a large healthy Japanese chestnut tree with vibrant green canopy and a smaller background tree, sunny sky — captioned 'The Japanese chestnut trees had evolved resistance to the fungus,'.

SVG: two-panel illustration. Top: large mature chestnut tree with diseased yellow-brown canopy, two orange zoom circles highlighting red-orange canker lesions on the trunk — captioned 'which causes chestnut blight disease in other tree species.'. Bottom: grid (8 columns x 4 rows) of bare, dead leafless chestnut tree silhouettes on white background — captioned '99 per cent of all American chestnut trees were killed within 50 years.'.

SVG: close-up botanical illustration of a chestnut tree trunk filling the frame, with vertical bark fissures and a large irregular orange-red sunken canker lesion (chestnut blight) centered on the trunk. Small dark fruiting bodies dot the lesion. Blurred green foliage background.
